updates.notes: Change.org petition gathered 2,800+ signatures. Planning and Zoning Commission voted against rezoning, Fiscal Court unanimously blocked proposal. County then adopted temporary moratorium on data center applications.

TenKey LandCo sued Simpson County over ordinance requiring heavy industrial zoning and conditional use permit. County Judge-Executive Mason Barnes led the ordinance. Fiscal Court filed response Feb 16 defending ordinance. Jasmine Butt gathered 1,200+ petition signatures on Change.org.

Franklin Planning and Zoning Commission tabled vote on TenKey's preliminary development plan twice (October 2025 and February 2026). Anonymity of end user drew passionate opposition. Town of less than 11,000 people. Proposed site at 421 Steele Road within Franklin city limits.

Change.org petition titled 'Oppose High Impact Data Center Development and Demand Sustainable Growth for Rockford, IL'. Monarch Energy finalizing $12 billion investment for data center near Chicago-Rockford International Airport across 1,100 acres. 200 high-tech jobs expected.

Change.org petition garnered 1,303 signatures citing air pollution, water quality, and tax impact concerns. Brennan Investment Group withdrew application 'based on initial feedback'. Village considers matter closed. Feb 2 plan commission meeting cancelled.

opposition_groups: Residents for Rural and Agricultural Land (RURAL)
updates.notes: RURAL group founded by Michelle and Erik Oftedahl and Sheila Monnier. Applied Digital's $16B 430MW project in Deuel County contingent on state tax exemption. Tax exemption bill killed in legislature. Company waiting to close on land. Over 100 people attended town hall.

SB 232 proposed one-year moratorium on hyperscale data center construction/expansion. Introduced Feb 4, 2026. Killed in Senate State Affairs Committee Feb 19. Separate bill SB 135 passed Senate with data center regulations but without tax incentive ban.

opposition_groups: Lincoln County residents (Commissioner Joel Arends supporters)
updates.notes: Moratorium motion by Commissioner Arends died for lack of a second on Feb 26, 2026. 15 residents spoke in favor of moratorium during 2-hour meeting. Commissioners Schmidt and Otten opposed moratorium. Public overwhelmingly supported pause but commission rejected it.

Board of Supervisors passed moratorium unanimously without debate. Moratorium expires November 8, 2026. Applies to rezoning, site plans, and building permits for data centers in unincorporated areas. County developing extensive zoning rules for data centers.

Commissioners unanimously approved moratorium through end of 2028 on data centers in unincorporated areas. No current proposed projects. County sits on Equus Beds Aquifer. Hyperscale centers can use 1M+ gallons/day. Proactive moratorium for research and regulation.

opposition_groups: Renwick Area Against Data Centers in West Sedgwick County
updates.notes: 90-day resolution adopted, expires April 17, 2026. Monarch Energies (Sacramento) had neighbors signing NDAs near Andale/Colwich in late 2025. Senate Majority Leader Chase Blasi called for 3-year moratorium. Town halls scheduled. Flood of opposition emails and calls.

City seeking rezone from Rural Residential to General Industrial for 300 acres. No end user identified but residents fear data center. ~45 directly notified, ~300 homes impacted. Public hearing rescheduled to March 25, 2026. Property annexed December 2025.

opposition_groups: Clean Air Coalition of Western New York
updates.notes: Two-year moratorium on new crypto mining and data center expansion passed unanimously July 2024. Digihost operates at former Fortistar gas plant on Erie Avenue since Feb 2022 with 4,000 bitcoin mining computers. On track to emit 300,000+ tons of GHG. Residents filed lawsuit. Deborah Gondek led organizing. Digihost later proposed nuclear microreactor partnership with NANO Nuclear Energy; city considering nuclear-free zone.

First town in New York to outlaw data centers and cryptocurrency mines. Zoning update passed unanimously Feb 19, 2026. Preceded by 18-month moratorium from July 2024. Dryden Climate Action Committee wrote letter advocating ban. Overwhelming public opposition at December and January hearings. Nearby Lansing had TeraWulf data center proposal at old coal plant.

Three-year moratorium on data centers and crypto mining adopted Dec 12, 2024 by 4-3 vote. Planning & Zoning Board passed moratoriums unanimously. 200+ people packed meeting. Residents wanted more information about who's building, who's buying, timeline. Mike Henke spoke in favor of moratorium.

opposition_groups: New Jersey League of Conservation Voters
updates.notes: Bill requiring large power users (100MW+) to pay 85%+ of service costs. Governor Murphy conditionally vetoed initial transparency bill, requested study on impact. Committees approved revised bill Feb 2026. NJ utility rates already 20% above national average. NJBIA and operating engineers union opposed legislation.

Town Council introduced ordinance banning data centers from all zones. First reading passed. Councilmember Edward Saultz called data centers 'terrible things'. Second reading and hearing scheduled March 10. Aims to protect rural character and agricultural lands.

opposition_groups: DE-CLEAR (Delaware Data Center Coalition for Local, Environmental, and Accountable Regulation)
updates.notes: DNREC denied permit under Coastal Zone Act. Project Washington: 580 acres, 6M sq ft, 1.2GW. 500+ diesel fuel tanks storing 2.5M gallons of diesel triggered heavy industry classification. Starwood appealed Feb 25. Councilman David Carter and Sen. Stephanie Hansen praised denial. Dustyn Thompson (Sierra Club Delaware) called it 'monumental win'.

Same Project Washington as New Castle County entry. DNREC Secretary Greg Patterson ruled project prohibited under Coastal Zone Act. Starwood appealed to Coastal Zone Industrial Control Board. Councilman Kevin Caneco praised decision. Dale Swain (local advocate) active in opposition.

Project Range: $14B, 1,000 acres, 30 industrial buildings, 5.6M sq ft near Yuma and Perryville roads. Tract (Denver-based) withdrew rezoning request May 2024. Cities of Goodyear and Buckeye sent letters citing incompatibility with residential surroundings. Later pursued revised project near Buckeye airport with local support.

Same Project Range as Maricopa County entry. Both cities sent official opposition letters. Goodyear and Buckeye city staff outlined concerns about building heights, noise, and emergency resources. Site surrounded by neighborhoods zoned for homes.

opposition_groups: Arizona Chapter of 7x24 Exchange (industry opposition to restrictions)
updates.notes: City Council approved zoning amendments July 9, 2025 defining data centers and restricting them to general/heavy industrial zones. 15 data centers built or proposed in 6 years including Google's $600M facility. Dozens of residents supported restrictions. Industry rep Tom Maples (7x24 Exchange) called rules 'overly restrictive'.

opposition_groups: No Data Center in Landover coalition
Party for Socialism and Liberation DC
updates.notes: 180-day moratorium approved Sept 2025. Taylor Frazier McCollum's petition against Landover Mall data center garnered 22,500+ signatures. County Executive Braveboy established task force. Moratorium extended to April 30, 2026. Proposed facility would use twice the water of Landover residents. Majority-Black community raising environmental justice concerns.

opposition_groups: Maryland Data Center Reform Coalition (Nature Forward)
updates.notes: Council voted 5-2 to approve Critical Digital Infrastructure Overlay Zone (under 1% of county land, ~4,200 acres). Sugarloaf Alliance (Steve Black, president) and Nature Forward coalition opposing expansion. Residents pushing for referendum, need ~15,000 signatures. Concerns about proximity to Carroll Manor and Ballenger Creek elementary schools. Maryland imports 40% of power.

opposition_groups: Troup County Anti-Data Center Coalition (Gage Bailey, founder)
updates.notes: 90-day moratorium set Sept 2025, extended additional 90 days in December. Hogansville also approved 90-day moratorium. LaGrange created 180-day moratorium. 437-acre Hogansville site proposed by Fertile Ground Investments (Luis Matta). Only 30-35 permanent jobs expected from 600MW project. Councilman Michael Taylor Jr. openly opposed.

Commissioner James Jenkins motioned moratorium in September 2025 to 'create a better understanding of how to accommodate data centers'. Part of broader Georgia wave of moratoriums (Lamar, Troup, Clayton, LaGrange also passed moratoriums in Sept). Lack of building codes/zoning for these facilities.

opposition_groups: Concerned Citizens of Twiggs County (Rob Fricks, attorney)
updates.notes: Lawsuit filed in Twiggs County Superior Court after commission unanimously rezoned 300 acres Sept 18, 2025. Nancy Lubeck among plaintiffs. Alleges wrong property/wrong date for notice sign, no Developments of Regional Impact review. Also alleges failure to evaluate black bear population impact and Muscogee Nation sacred land status. Eagle Rock (Charlotte, NC) estimates $2B taxable value. Property owned by Weyerhaeuser. Construction can't begin until 2028.

Stream Data Centers $2.4B 'Project Liberty' approved during winter storm Jan 2026. Council signed NDA, voted on all three readings hastily. ~100 residents attended opposition meeting. 'We feel like we were betrayed by our decision makers' - Jessica Thomas. Only 20 permanent jobs. Council had labeled it 'Project Liberty' without data center details.

_comment: === TASK 2: STATUS UPDATES ON MAJOR FIGHTS ===

updates.notes: STATUS UPDATE (Jan-Mar 2026): Ground broken on 1.2GW campus. SB Energy (SoftBank subsidiary) selected to build/operate. OpenAI and SoftBank each invested $500M into SB Energy. SB Energy acquired Studio 151 construction firm. Initial facilities expected to enter service starting 2026. Designed to minimize water usage. New generation planned to protect Texas ratepayers.

STATUS UPDATE (Jan-Mar 2026): First two buildings (980K sq ft, 200MW+) operational since Sept 2025, serving Oracle Cloud for OpenAI. Final building of Phase 1 topped out. Phase 2 construction underway: 6 more buildings, total 4M sq ft, 1.2GW capacity. Each building supports up to 50,000 NVIDIA GB200 NVL72s. ~2,000 workers on site daily, expected to reach 5,000. Phase 2 completion expected mid-2026.

STATUS UPDATE (Jan-Mar 2026): Kenosha unanimously approved rezoning Dec 2024. 240 acres purchased for 4 data center buildings (250K sq ft each). Part of $7B+ total Wisconsin investment. First datacenter at Mt. Pleasant on track for early 2026. Additional $4B committed for second campus. Community opposition ongoing -- packed Common Council meeting Nov 2025, residents called data centers 'parasites'. Viral TikTok sparked wave of concern. NDA with Microsoft frustrated residents.

STATUS UPDATE (Jan-Mar 2026): Township settled lawsuit with Related Digital, paving way for 2.2M sq ft data center on 575 acres. Original 4-1 denial overturned via settlement (potential $25M+ damages cited). Resident Kathryn Haushalter's motion to intervene citing Open Meetings Act violation denied by judge Feb 2026. Project now part of Stargate. DTE energy contracts approved by regulators.

STATUS UPDATE (Jan-Mar 2026): $100B 'Project Kestrel' planned at KCI-29 industrial site, 6 hyperscale data centers, 1.8M sq ft. Construction set to begin 2026. Presiding Commissioner Scott Fricker opposes. Utility rate concerns -- Evergy's 13.4% rate increase attributed to generation expansion for data centers. Note: 'Shenandoah Computing' name not found in sources -- may be different project or alias.

STATUS UPDATE (Jan-Mar 2026): $15B Vantage Data Centers project on 672-acre campus under construction. Will be called 'Lighthouse' -- OpenAI/Oracle Stargate site. 4 buildings planned, completion by 2028. Key battle over ATC transmission line route: Fredonia vs Saukville alternatives. PSC to decide spring 2026. Residents protested tax district. Eminent domain fears. Speeding/noise complaints from construction traffic.

STATUS UPDATE (Jan-Mar 2026): TCEQ granted nation's second-largest Clean Air permit Feb 2026. Over $600M in institutional equipment commitments secured in 30 days. $200M equipment facility from Keystone/Cape. Geotechnical work begun. Targeting 1GW delivery by end of 2026, then 2.3GW. Co-founded by Rick Perry. Partnership with Texas Tech University System.

STATUS UPDATE (Jan 2026): County Commissioners unanimously approved Jan 14, 2026. 600-acre data center + 659-acre power plant south of Cheyenne. 1.8GW initial, scalable to 10GW. Could become largest single AI campus in US. $50B+ total capex. Tallgrass building power plant (BFC Power/Cheyenne Power Hub). Up to 5,000 construction workers. First buildings potentially operational mid-2027. Would double Wyoming's current energy generation.

STATUS UPDATE (Jan-Mar 2026): Applied Digital's $16B project effectively stalled. 50-year sales tax exemption bill killed by legislature. Company says it won't close on land without exemption. Separate SB 232 moratorium bill also killed. SB 135 regulation bill passed Senate but stripped of tax incentive ban. Project future uncertain.

STATUS UPDATE (Feb 2026): Planning Commission voted 5-2 to grant preliminary site plan approval Feb 11, 2026. 'Project Cannoli': 282 acres, 1GW, 2-3.6M gallons water/day. Still needs Township Board development agreement approval, then final site plan. Panattoni hopes to break ground by end of 2026. End user not disclosed. Would be largest taxpayer in township and top-5 in Wayne County. Residents concerned about noise, water, utility costs, wildlife, property values.
